2603.17067.title: Evaluating Ill-Defined Tasks in Large Language Models
2603.17067.paper_summary: Many evaluations of Large Language Models (LLMs) target tasks that are inherently ill-defined, with unclear input and output spaces and ambiguous success criteria. We analyze why existing evaluation benchmarks and metrics fail to provide reliable or diagnostic signals of model capability for such tasks. We examine two case studies: Complex Instruction Following (CIF), where we identify recurring issues including limited coverage of real-world instruction complexity, sensitivity to instruction phrasing, inconsistent and non-comparable metrics, and instability introduced by LLM-based judges; and Natural Language to Mermaid Sequence Diagrams (NL2Mermaid), where we show how multi-faceted evaluation criteria can yield actionable insights beyond aggregate scores. Together, these case studies show that current evaluations frequently conflate distinct failure modes, yielding scores that are unstable, non-diagnostic, and difficult to act upon. Our findings expose fundamental limitations in existing evaluation practices for ill-defined tasks and motivate more robust, interpretable evaluation designs.
2603.17067.paper_summary_zh: 許多對大型語言模型（LLMs）的評估針對的是本質上不明確的任務，這些任務的輸入和輸出空間不清晰，成功標準模糊不清。<lb/>我們分析了為什麼現有的評估基準和指標未能為這類任務提供可靠或診斷性的模型能力信號。<lb/>我們檢視了兩個案例研究：複雜指令遵循（CIF），在這裡我們識別出重複出現的問題，包括對現實世界指令複雜性的覆蓋有限、對指令措辭的敏感性、不一致且無法比較的指標，以及由LLM基礎的評判者引入的不穩定性；以及自然語言到美人魚序列圖（NL2Mermaid），在這裡我們展示了多面向的評估標準如何產生超越總體分數的可行見解。<lb/>這些案例研究共同表明，當前的評估經常混淆不同的失敗模式，產生不穩定、無法診斷且難以採取行動的分數。<lb/>我們的發現揭示了現有評估實踐在不明確任務中的基本限制，並激勵更健全、可解釋的評估設計。
2603.17067.author: Yi Zhou et.al.
